seasonArc:
Adam Yates entered 2026 as UAE Team Emirates-XRG's secondary climbing card behind Pogačar — the protected GC-grade lieutenant when Pogačar was racing, the team's protected outright leader when he wasn't. His spring tactical role had been visible without being headline-making: support work through the Tour de Suisse build-up, a Giro start as one of the team's two GC cards.

Then Stage 2 in Bulgaria happened. The rain-soaked descent into Veliko Tarnovo, ~21-23 km to go, a mass crash that took out four UAE riders at once. Yates hit a metal guardrail, finished the stage with blood on his face and over 13 minutes down. Initially cleared for concussion at the roadside, withdrew the next morning after delayed concussive symptoms emerged. The Giro that was meant to be Yates's quiet support-and-stage-hunting ride became the second-shortest Giro of his career — one stage finished, one stage's worth of injuries, the head trauma still under management at compile time.

healthStatus.currentSummary: Out since 10 May 2026 (delayed concussive symptoms diagnosed the morning after a Giro Stage 2 crash). Heavy abrasions, laceration to left ear, 'important cut to his head' (Bernie Eisel quote). Initially cleared at the roadside for concussion, withdrew the following day. Part of the same Bulgarian descent crash that took out Vine (broken elbow), Soler (fractured pelvis), and added to UAE's mid-May injury crisis. No surgery reported; no public return date.

timeline.label: Giro Stage 2 crash — Veliko Tarnovo descent
timeline.description: Crashed in a mass pile-up on a rain-soaked descent, ~21-23 km to go. Hit a metal guardrail, suffered facial injuries and a head wound, finished the stage with blood and dirt on his face, 13+ minutes down. UAE's worst day of 2026 — five riders in the same crash.

timeline.label: Concussion — DNS Stage 3
timeline.description: UAE medical director Dr. Adrian Rotunno statement: 'He was initially assessed on-site for concussion and cleared to continue, but subsequently has shown delayed concussive symptoms. He will not take the start of stage 3 today.' Heavy abrasions, laceration to left ear, important cut to head.

timeline.label: Heading home for rehab
timeline.description: Team statement: 'All three [Yates, Vine, Soler] are under observation of our medical staff and will travel home in the coming days to continue their recovery and rehab.' No specified return-to-racing date.

healthStatus.expectedReturn: TBD — head injury rehab, no public timeline
